query.help: Search for items on Pipedrive. Example: pipedrive search query "my search term" --limit 20 --table pipedrive search query "keyword" --filter "price:lt:100" pipedrive search query "keyword" --properties "title,price"
query.usage_instructions: Search Pipedrive items using a free-text query. Pass the required query text, `--limit/-l` to cap results, `--filter/-f` for client-side filtering, `--table/-t` for tabular output, and `--properties/-p` to select fields.

item.help: Get details for a specific item. Example: pipedrive search item abc123 --table pipedrive search item "https://example.com/item/abc123" pipedrive search item abc123 --properties "title,price"
item.usage_instructions: Get one Pipedrive item by ID or URL. Pass the required item identifier, add `--table/-t` for a key-value table, and use `--properties/-p` to limit the returned fields.

list.help: List items from Pipedrive. Example: pipedrive search list --limit 100 --table pipedrive search list --filter "status:active" pipedrive search list --properties "id,name,status"
list.usage_instructions: List Pipedrive items from the scaffolded browser source. Use `--limit/-l` to cap items, `--filter/-f` for client-side filtering, `--table/-t` for tabular output, and `--properties/-p` to select fields.

wildcard.help: Search items with wildcard pattern matching. Supports * wildcards for pattern matching across all string fields. Browser CLIs use client-side search.
wildcard.usage_instructions: Run client-side wildcard matching against scaffolded Pipedrive items. Pass a required wildcard query, use `--fields` to scope matching, `--limit/-l` to cap the scan, `--table/-t` for tabular output, and `--properties/-p` to select fields.

search.usage_instructions: Generic scaffold search surface for Pipedrive. Use these commands to query, list, inspect, or wildcard-match items once the browser parser and client methods are implemented for the target site.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Open or refresh the saved browser session for Pipedrive. Use `--profile/-p` for a named profile, `--force/-F` to clear and re-authenticate, and `--credential-type/-c` only when you need to target one credential type.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Clear saved `pipedrive` credentials and browser session data. Use `--profile/-p` to remove one profile instead of all saved sessions.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test`` (API/OAuth) or via the live browser check (browser_session).

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Check whether saved Pipedrive credentials are present and authenticated. Use `--profile/-p` to inspect one profile or `--table/-t` for a compact multi-profile view.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Verify that the saved browser session for Pipedrive is still usable. Use `--profile/-p` to scope the check, `--table/-t` for summary output, and `--verbose/-v` for per-check details.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List saved `pipedrive` authentication profiles. Use `--table/-t` for tabular output, `--limit/-l` to cap the result set, `--filter/-f` to narrow profiles, and `--properties/-p` to select fields.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Show details for one saved `pipedrive` profile. Pass the profile name as the required argument and add `--table/-t` for a key-value table view.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a named `pipedrive` profile from the template config. Use when you need a separate saved session for another Pipedrive account or environment.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Mark one `pipedrive` profile as the default profile. Use when future auth and data commands should use that profile unless `--profile/-p` is passed explicitly.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a named `pipedrive` profile and its stored data. Use when a saved session is no longer needed or must be recreated cleanly.

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage named authentication profiles for `pipedrive`. Use these commands when you need separate saved sessions for different Pipedrive accounts or environments.

auth.usage_instructions: Manage browser-session authentication for Pipedrive. Use `auth login` to create or refresh a session, `auth status` or `auth test` to verify it, and `auth profiles ...` to manage named saved sessions.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Delete cached `pipedrive` responses for the active profile. Use this when you need fresh results instead of cached browser-authenticated reads.

cache.usage_instructions: Inspect or clear cached `pipedrive` responses. Use cache commands when you need to confirm cache state or force fresh browser-authenticated reads.

usage_instructions: Use for browser-based Pipedrive operations through the `pipedrive` CLI. Commands follow `pipedrive <group> <action>`; run `pipedrive auth login` first, use `auth status` or `auth test` to verify the saved session, and use `search` commands after the site-specific parser is implemented. Pass `--no-cache` when you need an uncached browser-authenticated read.
